channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Persistent dog waste fly-tipping under bushes at Gadwall Way, 29th March
item.description:
I am reporting an emerging issue with dog waste bags being discarded under the bushes near SE28 0DB. Currently, there are at least 7 bags scattered around this specific spot. While there is no strong odor yet, this is clearly becoming a "hotspot" for irresponsible dog owners to dump their waste instead of using a bin. If this is not cleared immediately, it will encourage others to do the same, leading to a much larger health hazard and litter problem. Request: 1. Please arrange for these 7+ bags to be removed before the pile grows. 2. I would appreciate a "No Littering/Dog Fouling" sign or a temporary notice to deter this behavior at this specific location. Thank you for maintaining our local green space.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Gadwall Way, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Garden umbrella and settee dumped in river, 29th March
item.description:
Someone has dumped a broken garden umbrella and a wicker garden settee in the river

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Charter Drive, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light doesn’t work, 29th March
item.description:
Street light doesn’t work for over 6 months .Problem was reported twice last year .Still not resolved

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wren Path, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stembridge Road, Anerley, Greater London

item.title: Near tree and path where people walk, 29th March
item.description:
The problem is people keep flytipping this as reported over the years and you do absolutely nothing terrible. Are you gonna get the culprits ? There's a camera look and do some thing. Crime is going on and I will keep going on.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Church Manorway, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped paint and rubbish, 29th March
item.description:
Box of stuff and hazardous waste (paint) left by householder on street. Please can it be cleared and the council have a word with occupants (given Hackney is expanding enforcement for fly tipping)

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Warneford Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bin Store overflowing, 29th March
item.description:
I work for the london firebrigade and have attended this incident. Bins are overflowing in every bin store on the Ignatius sancho estate in all the different blocks. The LFB were called to attend due to the fire risk of so much fire loading rubbish that would cause big problems. If a fire were to occur, It puts residents and the LFB personnel at risk due to the amount of rubbish building up.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ignatius Sancho Road, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipping, 29th March
item.description:
Please see the photo. (This is another episode of recurrent fly-tipping by residents of the adjacent house).

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Church Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bad repair to pavement, 29th March
item.description:
Outside of the Playhouse in Erith High Street some broken paving stones have been repaired. Unfortunately the repair is of a very poor quality. It looks like a couple of handfuls of tarmac have been dropped in the cracks and left. This has resulted in the pavement being in a worse state than before the repair. One paving slab is at least 2 inches above the rest on one corner which is a trip hazard. The quality of the repair is very very poor
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Erith High Street, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shoe rack flytipped, 29th March
